title: BLDP Path to Profitability and Financial Targets
text: Ballard Power Systems targets cash flow breakeven by end of 2027 following multi-year transformation. Key profitability drivers include gross margin expansion (improving from -32% in 2024 to 14% in Q1 2026), operating expense reduction (30%+ achieved), and revenue growth. Margin improvement through product cost reduction targets 40% cost per megawatt savings by 2027. Commercial terms improvement includes higher-margin fleet services, long-term service agreements, and integrated solution pricing. Operating expense targets of $65-75M annually in 2026 represent 40%+ reduction from 2024 levels. Cash operating cost reductions of 41% in Q4 2025 vs Q4 2024 demonstrate structural cost improvements. Revenue expansion to $118M+ in 2026 provides operating leverage toward breakeven. Full-year 2025 revenue of $99M+ represents 43% YoY growth trajectory supporting profitability inflection. Adjusted EBITDA improved to negative $11.4M in Q1 2026 from negative $27.5M in Q1 2025, indicating progress toward positive EBITDA. Management guidance emphasizes disciplined execution on margin expansion and cost control as critical to achieving profitability targets. The 2027 cash flow breakeven goal provides explicit financial commitment and market signal of management confidence in business model sustainability.
